criteria.name: Projection Quality
criteria.1: No rest-of-season projections pulled, used season-to-date counting stats as proxy, or used only one unverified source without a URL
criteria.3: FanGraphs ATC rest-of-season projections pulled for all players involved, source URLs cited, one or two players missing minor fields (e.g., QS projection estimated by heuristic)
criteria.5: FanGraphs ATC rest-of-season (atcr) pulled for every player with source URL cited, fallback (Depth Charts or Razzball) cross-checked where ATC and roles diverge, IL status verified on RotoWire, any projection >15% discrepancy between sources uses the conservative value with rationale

criteria.name: Counting Category Math
criteria.1: Counting deltas computed from full-season not ROS, or sign errors (e.g., IN minus OUT reversed), or cats missing entirely
criteria.3: All seven counting cats (R, HR, RBI, SB, K, QS, SV) computed from ROS projections with correct IN-minus-OUT subtraction, table displayed both teams side by side
criteria.5: All counting cats computed from ROS ATC, table shows raw deltas both our side and mirror (their side), SV projections validated against save_role_certainty from player-analyzer, QS projections are directly pulled (not heuristic) from FanGraphs

criteria.name: Ratio Category Math
criteria.1: Simple arithmetic differences of team-level OBP/ERA/WHIP averages used (wrong), or ratio cats omitted entirely
criteria.3: Volume-weighted computation attempted for OBP, ERA, WHIP (PA or IP as denominator), minor scaling errors or sign confusion on ERA/WHIP
criteria.5: Ratio cats weighted by PA (OBP) or IP (ERA, WHIP) using implied-components method (ER, baserunners), sign correctly flipped for ERA and WHIP so positive contribution to trade_cat_delta means trade helps us, scaling to common units documented (OBP×10000, ERA×50, WHIP×200)

criteria.name: cat_pressure Weighting
criteria.1: Flat (uniform 1.0) weights used across all 10 cats, or cat_pressure not read from the category-state signal file
criteria.3: cat_pressure read from the most recent cat-state signal, weight = pressure/50 applied to each cat, weighted sum computed
criteria.5: cat_pressure read from today's cat-state signal file with source file path cited, weight formula (pressure/50) applied to each of the 10 cats, weighted sum computed and displayed alongside raw sum, any cat with pressure>=80 flagged as a forced-reject trigger if its delta is negative, mirror-side uses raw (not our) pressures with rationale

criteria.name: trade_value_delta Dollar Calculation
criteria.1: No dollar value computed, or a made-up number not sourced from an auction calculator
criteria.3: FanGraphs Auction Calculator values pulled for each player, net delta computed and bucketed as gain/noise/loss
criteria.5: FanGraphs AC values configured for 12-team H2H OBP-variant 5x5 league, pulled for all players with source URL, ROS version used, value bucketed into the five calibration tiers (>=+$10 through <=-$10), injury discount applied for any IL player with >4 weeks of expected missed time, fallback to Razzball with OBP adjustment documented when FG unavailable

criteria.name: positional_flex_delta Scoring
criteria.1: Position impact not evaluated, or scored as binary up/down with no rationale
criteria.3: Position flex scored on a -100 to +100 scale using scarcity of positions (C, SS scarce; OF abundant) and bench/slot impacts, total computed
criteria.5: Each contributing factor scored individually per the scoring anchors (scarce-position gain/loss, multi-position eligibility, bench consolidation, SP/RP count alignment with starting slots), contributions summed and clamped to [-100, +100], table in the template fully populated, written in beginner-voice ("gain depth at SS where we had only two eligible players")

criteria.name: playoff_impact Computation
criteria.1: playoff_impact skipped or set to arbitrary value regardless of date
criteria.3: Pre-July set to 50/neutral with note, July+ attempts computation using team schedules
criteria.5: Date-gated (pre-July = 50 with N/A note), July 1-14 computed at normal weight, July 15+ computed and double-weighted in verdict, games×matchup_quality pulled from mlb-playoff-scheduler signal with source URL, pitcher playoff_games converted to projected STARTS (not team games) via Roster Resource probables grid, clamped to 0-100 with 50 neutral anchor

criteria.name: Verdict Calibration and Reject Bias
criteria.1: Verdict not one of the three enums (ACCEPT/COUNTER/REJECT), or verdict contradicts the signal values, or biased toward ACCEPT
criteria.3: Verdict correctly maps to one of the three enums using threshold logic, acknowledges reject bias in ambiguous cases
criteria.5: Decision tree applied explicitly: all five ACCEPT conditions checked and logged (value>=+$5, weighted cat delta>+30, no high-pressure cat negative, flex>=0, playoff>=50 if applicable); forced-REJECT overrides (any pressure>=80 cat negative, value<=-$10, flex<=-30, July 15+ playoff<=40, IL uncertain) explicitly evaluated and triggered where applicable; tie goes to reject; rationale explains which condition(s) drove the verdict

criteria.name: Counter-Offer Specificity
criteria.1: Verdict is COUNTER but no specific counter-package is proposed (just 'ask for more'), or counter is obviously extractive and will be rejected
criteria.3: Counter proposes a specific named player to add or swap, identifies the gap the counter closes
criteria.5: Counter identifies the specific gap (value short by $X, or cat-C negative, or flex marginal), scans opponent's actual Yahoo roster for plausible candidates, proposes a specific named player (not a tier), estimates acceptance probability with one sentence of rationale, pre-commits to REJECT if counter is refused

criteria.name: Documentation and Signal Compliance
criteria.1: No signal file written, or output not in the template schema, or no source URLs, or no confidence score
criteria.3: Signal file written to signals/YYYY-MM-DD-trade-<opponent>.md with frontmatter and major fields populated, source URLs cited for projections
criteria.5: Signal file fully compliant with the signal-framework schema (type: trade, all required fields), every projection and dollar value has a source URL, confidence computed and >=0.5 for a final verdict (or <0.5 with explicit note), red_team_findings block populated with at least one finding, decision log entry emitted via mlb-decision-logger with will_verify_on date set to 4 weeks out, output written in beginner-voice with jargon translated inline, ends with a single-verb recommendation

key_requirements: Be especially skeptical: opponent is consolidating, which implies they expect the concentration to help them
Check whether the players we give up are genuinely close to waiver-tier; if they are actually startable, that's hidden value
Positional flex often tips negative on these offers because we free bench slots the opponent wanted to free for themselves
common_pitfalls: Letting the headline star's name override the math
Treating our own players as more droppable than they are
Ignoring that opponent frees a roster spot to stream from waivers

key_requirements: Read cat_pressure carefully: does the swap match OUR pressures or THEIRS?
Only accept when gained cats have pressure >=70 AND given-up cats have pressure <=40
Cross-check the mirror table — if their raw cat deltas also improve their needs, the trade may still be extractive for us
common_pitfalls: Assuming category swaps are automatically fair because both sides address weaknesses
Using today's standings instead of rest-of-season projections (standings are noisy in April/May)

key_requirements: Cross-reference mlb-regression-flagger signal for each player's regression_index
If their player's regression_index is sharply positive (unlucky, bounce-back due) AND ours is sharply negative (lucky, regression incoming), a flat-dollar trade is a gain — ACCEPT is justified
Document the regression thesis in the signal file so it can be verified in 4 weeks
common_pitfalls: Anchoring on season-to-date results when xwOBA / xERA tell a different story
Assuming the opponent is equally sophisticated about regression (they may or may not be)

key_requirements: RotoWire injury report check is step 1, before any other analysis
If projected return costs >4 weeks, manually scale the ATC projection — do not trust the ensemble to have repriced
Default to REJECT because the ATC staleness is common and the information asymmetry favors the offerer
common_pitfalls: Trusting ATC for a player who went on IL in the last 7 days
Underestimating the psychological pressure on the injured player's team to offload (they know more than us)

key_requirements: Pull weeks 21-23 game counts per team from mlb.com/schedule, not the generic team season schedule
For SPs, convert to projected starts using Roster Resource probables grid — pitchers do not start every team game
Double-weight playoff_impact in the verdict for trades proposed July 15 or later
common_pitfalls: Counting team games as pitcher starts (overstates SP playoff contribution by ~60%)
Not flagging trade deadline proximity (Aug 6) — a bad trade this late cannot be reversed

common_failure_modes.symptom: A player with 20 HR in April is valued as if he'll hit 40+ over the full season, independent of games remaining
common_failure_modes.detection: Check the FanGraphs URL — does it end in 'atc' (full season) or 'atcr' (rest of season)?
common_failure_modes.fix: Switch to atcr. Re-pull all projections. This typically changes trade_value_delta by $5-$15 for hot/cold starters.

common_failure_modes.symptom: OBP delta reported as (AVG(OBP_IN)) - (AVG(OBP_OUT)) without weighting
common_failure_modes.detection: Compute weighted version; if the result differs by more than 0.002 from the simple average, the simple version is wrong
common_failure_modes.fix: Weight by PA (OBP) or IP (ERA, WHIP) per the ratio category math section. Use implied-components approach (sum of ER or baserunners divided by IP).

common_failure_modes.symptom: Every cat's delta contributes equally to the weighted sum; trade verdict doesn't reflect user's actual weekly needs
common_failure_modes.detection: Open the cat-state signal; if pressures vary meaningfully (range > 30) but the weighted sum equals the raw sum, weighting wasn't applied
common_failure_modes.fix: Read signals/YYYY-MM-DD-cat-state.md. Apply weight = pressure/50 to each cat before summing. Re-render verdict.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Verdict is COUNTER but the output does not name a specific player to request
common_failure_modes.detection: Grep the output for a named player in the counter-package section
common_failure_modes.fix: Pull the opponent's Yahoo roster. Identify a specific player that closes the quantified gap. Propose that player by name and pre-commit to REJECT if refused.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Incoming player is projected at full value but is currently on the IL
common_failure_modes.detection: Cross-check RotoWire injury report for every IN player before finalizing the verdict
common_failure_modes.fix: If the player is on IL and ATC hasn't repriced, scale the ROS projection manually. If return date is unknown, flag as forced REJECT.

common_failure_modes.symptom: A trade that gains a better pitcher shows a negative weighted delta in ERA or WHIP
common_failure_modes.detection: Spot-check: if IN pitchers have lower projected ERA than OUT pitchers, the contribution to trade_cat_delta must be positive
common_failure_modes.fix: Multiply the raw ratio-cat delta by -1 before scaling and summing. Lower ERA/WHIP is better; the sign convention must make that a positive contribution.

common_failure_modes.failure: Accept bias instead of reject bias
common_failure_modes.symptom: A trade that is close to fair (value_delta in -$5 to +$5 band) is accepted instead of countered
common_failure_modes.detection: Re-read the decision tree: ACCEPT requires value_delta >= +$5, not just >= 0
common_failure_modes.fix: If value_delta < +$5, the verdict cannot be ACCEPT. It is COUNTER if the other conditions are met, or REJECT otherwise.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Verdict is ACCEPT despite a cat with pressure >=80 having a negative weighted delta
common_failure_modes.detection: Walk the forced-REJECT override list explicitly for each verdict
common_failure_modes.fix: Force REJECT. The trade sacrifices a category the user critically needs; no amount of dollar value gain compensates within a weekly H2H framework.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Mirror analysis uses our cat_pressure values on the opponent's side
common_failure_modes.detection: Check the mirror table: are the weights the same as our side?
common_failure_modes.fix: Either use raw (unweighted) deltas for the mirror, or pull the opponent's standings per-cat and derive their pressures separately.

common_failure_modes.symptom: A trade that is manifestly lopsided in our favor is accepted without noting that the commissioner may reverse it
common_failure_modes.detection: If trade_value_delta >= +$20, the trade is likely to be reviewed and potentially reversed in this league
common_failure_modes.fix: Add a red-team finding: 'Commissioner review may reverse this trade; consider restructuring to make it less lopsided on paper while preserving the gain.'
